pabbajitena.meaning: by one gone forth; by someone who has left the householder's life
pabbajitena.gram: past participle of pabbajati used as a noun, instrumental singular
breakdown: To go; pabbajati means to go forth from home into homelessness.
Past participle suffix; pabbajita is "one gone forth", a renunciate.

pabbajito.meaning: one gone forth; someone who has left the householder's life
pabbajito.gram: past participle of pabbajati used as a masculine noun, nominative singular
breakdown: To go; pabbajati means to go forth into homelessness.

pabbajja.meaning: going forth; leaving the world, adopting the life of an ascetic, getting ordained
pabbajja.gram: gerund of pabbajati (pa + √vaj); also the stem of the noun pabbajjā
breakdown: To go; the going forth from household life.

pabbajjā.meaning: the going forth; leaving the world to live as an ascetic or recluse, ordination

pabbatavisamaṁ.meaning: mountainous rough ground; uneven mountain terrain

pabhinnapaṭisambhide.meaning: developed in analytical insight; one in whom the analytical knowledges have unfolded
pabhinnapaṭisambhide.gram: adjective, compound, locative or vocative form
breakdown: Burst open, unfolded, developed (past participle of pabhindati).

pabujjhasi.gram: verb, 2nd person singular present of pabujjhati (pa + √budh)
breakdown: To awaken; pabujjhati means to wake up.
Prefix: against, back (paty- assimilates to pacc- before a vowel).
To declare; paccakkhāti means to reject, give up, disavow.

paccantimesu.meaning: in the border (regions); among the outlying, neighbouring (districts)

paccanubhossasī.meaning: you will undergo; you will experience
paccanubhossasī.gram: verb, 2nd person singular future of paccanubhavati (paṭi + anu + √bhū)
breakdown: To be; anubhavati means to experience, undergo.

paccassosuṁ.meaning: they replied; they assented, they agreed
paccassosuṁ.gram: verb, 3rd person plural aorist of paṭissuṇāti (paṭi + √su)
breakdown: To hear; paṭissuṇāti means to answer, assent.

paccattaṁ.meaning: individually; for oneself, each for himself
paccattaṁ.gram: adjective used adverbially, accusative singular (paṭi + atta)
breakdown: Self; paccattaṁ means "with reference to oneself", individually.
paccattaṁ.goenka: Paccattaṁ carries the experiential emphasis central to the tradition: the Dhamma is paccattaṁ veditabbo viññūhi, to be experienced directly by the wise, each for oneself. Truth is not accepted on another's authority but verified individually, within one's own mind and body.

paccavekkhaṇattho.meaning: for the purpose of reviewing; the aim of looking at (reflecting upon)
breakdown: Reviewing, looking at, reflection (paṭi + ava + √ikkh "to see").

paccavekkhitabbāni.meaning: to be contemplated; to be looked upon, to be considered
paccavekkhitabbāni.gram: future passive participle of paccavekkhati (paṭi + ava + √ikkh), neuter plural
breakdown: To see; paccavekkhati means to review, contemplate.

paccavekkhitvā.meaning: having considered; having contemplated, having reviewed

paccayadānasaññājanakaṁ.meaning: producing the idea of giving requisites; giving rise to the perception that requisites should be offered
breakdown: Requisite; the material supports of a monk's life.
Connected with, bound up with (past participle of paṭi + saṁ + √yuj).

paccayapaṭisevana.meaning: use of the requisites (in the source context explained as paccaya + paṭisedhana, refusal of requisites)
paccayapaṭisevana.note: uncertain: the lexicon notes that in its source passage the word stands for paccaya + paṭisedhana, "requisites + refusal".

paccayiko.gram: adjective, masculine nominative singular (from paccaya "ground, confidence")
breakdown: Ground, reason; confidence, trust (paṭi + √i "to go back to").

paccaññāsiṁ.meaning: I acknowledged; I agreed, I admitted
paccaññāsiṁ.gram: verb, 1st person singular aorist of paṭijānāti (paṭi + √ñā)
breakdown: To know; paṭijānāti means to acknowledge, admit, profess.

pacchimo.gram: adjective, masculine nominative singular (superlative of pacchā)

pacchimā.gram: adjective, feminine nominative singular or masculine plural of pacchima

pacchimābhimukhā.meaning: facing west; turned towards the west
pacchimābhimukhā.gram: adjective, compound, plural or feminine form
breakdown: Facing towards, turned to (abhi + mukha "face"). (The long ā at the junction arises by vowel coalescence.)

pacchābhattaṁ.meaning: after the meal; in the afternoon
pacchābhattaṁ.gram: indeclinable adverb, compound (accusative singular of pacchābhatta)

paccuggacchati.meaning: goes out to meet; leaves, goes out

paccuggamana.meaning: going out to meet; meeting, receiving (a guest)

paccupaṭṭhapethāti.meaning: "arrange, provide!"; have it made ready, wait upon (quoted command)
paccupaṭṭhapethāti.gram: verb, 2nd person plural imperative, causative of paccupaṭṭhahati, with quotation marker ti
breakdown: To stand; the causative means to set up, provide, wait upon.
Second person plural ending followed by the quotation marker ti (with vowel lengthening at the boundary).

paccupaṭṭhitaṁ.meaning: present; ready, imminent (literally "made to stand in front")
paccupaṭṭhitaṁ.gram: past participle of paccupaṭṭhāti, accusative/neuter singular
breakdown: To stand; paccupaṭṭhita means standing ready, present.

paccupaṭṭhitā.gram: past participle of paccupaṭṭhahati, plural or feminine form
breakdown: To stand; set up, made available.

paccuppannena.meaning: the present (literally "what has just arisen"); with the present moment
paccuppannena.gram: past participle of paccuppajjati used as a noun, instrumental singular
breakdown: To go; uppanna means arisen; paccuppanna is what has presently arisen, the present.

paccuṭṭheti.meaning: rises out of respect; stands up to greet
paccuṭṭheti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, causative of paccuṭṭhāti (paṭi + ud + √ṭhā)
breakdown: To stand; to rise up before someone as a mark of respect.

paccājāti.meaning: rebirth; being reborn, coming to a new existence
paccājāti.gram: feminine abstract noun (from paccājāyati, paṭi + ā + √jan)

paccājāto.meaning: reborn; come into a new existence
paccājāto.gram: past participle of paccājāyati, masculine nominative singular
breakdown: To be born; paccājāta means born again, come into existence.

paccājāyanti.meaning: they are reborn; they come into a new existence
paccājāyanti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present of paccājāyati

paccājāyati.meaning: is reborn; is born into the next existence

breakdown: To hope for, wish, expect (also written āsiṁsati; ā + √siṁs).

pacurā.gram: adjective, plural or feminine form of pacura

pada paramo.meaning: one for whom words are the utmost; one who grasps no more than the words
pada paramo.summary: This is the term padaparama, the last of the four types of persons classified by their capacity to understand the teaching.
breakdown: Utmost, highest; padaparama is literally "one for whom the words are the utmost".
pada paramo.goenka: The study material explains padaparama literally as "one for whom words are utmost": of the four kinds of listeners, the one who, however much he hears, learns and recites, gets no further than the words of the teaching in this life — in contrast to those of quick or unfolding understanding who penetrate the meaning. The classification underscores the tradition's emphasis that the words of the Dhamma are a vehicle; the goal is direct realization beyond them.

padahati.meaning: strives; exerts oneself, puts forth energy
breakdown: To put; padahati means to apply oneself, strive.

padakkhiṇaṁ.meaning: with the right side towards; circumambulation keeping one's right side to a person or object of respect
padakkhiṇaṁ.gram: adjective, accusative singular, used adverbially (pa + dakkhiṇa)
breakdown: Right (side); the respectful circuit is made keeping the right side towards the one honoured.

padakkhiṇaṁ katvā.meaning: having circumambulated with the right side towards; having paid respect by keeping one's right side to a person or object
breakdown: With the right side towards (pa + dakkhiṇa "right").

padaṁ.meaning: state; place, footing; word, verse-line; track
breakdown: To go, step; pada is the step, footing, place — and by extension the word or line as a "step" of text.

padhāniyaṅga.meaning: factor of endeavour; one of the five qualities required for successful striving
breakdown: Connected with exertion, fit for striving (from padhāna "exertion").
padhāniyaṅga.goenka: The study material lists the five padhāniyaṅgas, factors of endeavour, as saddho, appābādho, asaṭho, āraddhaviriyo, paññavā — faith, good health, honesty, tireless effort and wisdom. Klaus Nothnagel points to the Introduction to lesson 1.4.3, Dhajaggasuttaṁ — Verses for Protection, for further detail. These are the supports a meditator needs to work patiently and persistently on the path.

paduṭṭhena.meaning: impure; corrupt, spoilt (instrumental: with a corrupted mind)
paduṭṭhena.gram: past participle of padussati (pa + √dus), instrumental singular
breakdown: To spoil, corrupt; paduṭṭha means corrupted, defiled.
paduṭṭhena.goenka: The word opens the Dhammapada's first verse, cited in the study material: manasā ce paduṭṭhena bhāsati vā karoti vā — if one speaks or acts with an impure, corrupted mind, suffering follows like the wheel follows the foot of the ox. Purity and impurity of mind, not outward form, determine the result of action (kamma).

breakdown: Prefix: forth. (The doubled gg arises by assimilation.)
To take, seize; paggaṇhāti means to stretch forth, take up.

paggaṇhāti.meaning: takes up; stretches forth, holds up; supports
breakdown: Prefix: forth. (The doubled gg arises by assimilation.)

pahitattā.meaning: of resolute will; energetic, with self applied (to the goal)
pahitattā.gram: adjective, compound (pahita + atta), masculine nominative singular or plural
breakdown: Applied, directed, resolute (past participle of padahati "to strive").
Self; pahitatta is "one whose self is applied", resolute.
